question_text: A motor has an efficiency of 80%. If the motor is supplied with 500 J of electrical energy, calculate how much useful mechanical energy is produced and how much energy is wasted.
correct_answer: Useful mechanical energy = 400 J; Wasted energy = 100 J. Using efficiency = (useful energy output ÷ energy input) × 100%, we get: 80 = (useful energy ÷ 500) × 100, so useful energy = 400 J. Wasted energy = 500 - 400 = 100 J.
explanation: This requires students to apply the efficiency formula and perform multi-step calculations. Understanding that not all input energy becomes useful output is crucial for real-world applications.
